category: Guarded No-Op Freshness Uses Session-Loaded ContentVersion
description: Fix the guarded no-op path so freshness is validated against the ContentVersion observed by the executor's session-owned current-state load, not only the earlier repository target lookup. Acceptance: when an existing-document write loads current state and the hydrated `DocumentMetadata.ContentVersion` is newer than the repository lookup stamp, the executor updates the active existing-target context before guarded no-op freshness revalidation; unchanged PUT and POST-as-update requests do not return `StaleNoOpCompare` or `WriteConflict` solely because the document changed before the current-state load but remained unchanged afterward; the current-state loader's `DocumentMetadata` is no longer dead data for freshness decisions; repository stale retry still occurs only when freshness is actually lost after compare, and not because of a stale pre-session lookup value.
steps-to-verify: Add backend unit coverage for an existing-document guarded no-op where repository lookup observes one `ContentVersion`, current-state load observes a newer `ContentVersion`, and the freshness check sees that newer value still current; assert the executor returns `GuardedNoOp` instead of `StaleNoOpCompare`.

Add or update backend unit coverage showing the executor rewrites the active `RelationalWriteTargetContext.ExistingDocument.ObservedContentVersion` from the session-loaded `DocumentMetadata.ContentVersion` before calling `IRelationalWriteFreshnessChecker`.

Add or update repository unit coverage proving the single stale-no-op retry still happens only when freshness is truly lost after compare, and does not trigger solely because the pre-session lookup stamp was older than the session-loaded current-state stamp.

Add PostgreSQL integration coverage for an unchanged PUT or POST-as-update where the target changes before the write attempt begins but remains stable after current-state load; assert success, no resource-table DML, and unchanged `dms.Document.ContentVersion` during the no-op attempt.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DefaultRelationalWriteExecutorTests|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepositoryTests|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalWriteGuardedNoOpTests|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalWriteCurrentStateLoaderTests"`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~PostgresqlRelationalWriteGuardedNoOpTests"`

description: Correct the client-visible error classification for immutable-identity failures on POST-as-update. Acceptance: when a POST resolves to an existing document and the merged identity projection changes for a resource with `allowIdentityUpdates=false`, the relational backend no longer returns `UpsertResult.UnknownFailure`; the request fails through an explicit immutable-identity result contract rather than a generic system-error path, a new `UpsertResult.UpsertFailureImmutableIdentity` with the same client-visible response behavior in core as UpdateResult.UpdateFailureImmutableIdentity on the PUT path; `UpsertFailureIdentityConflict` is not reused for this update-time immutable-identity case; the core upsert handler no longer maps this case to HTTP 500; no relational DML is committed for the rejected request.
steps-to-verify: Update backend unit tests for `RelationalWriteIdentityStability` or `DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor` to assert POST-as-update immutable-identity failures do not return `UnknownFailure` and instead use the chosen explicit immutable-identity result contract.

Add or update core handler tests for the upsert path to assert the frontend response for this case is not HTTP 500, uses the chosen immutable-identity mapping, and carries the expected immutable-identity detail.

Add an integration test for relational POST-as-update with an identity-changing request against an existing document with `allowIdentityUpdates=false`; assert failure classification, no committed row changes, and no misleading system-error response.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DefaultRelationalWriteExecutorTests"`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~UpsertHandlerTests"`

description: Narrow the duplicated existing-target lookup orchestration so repository-owned initial lookup and executor-owned POST re-evaluation cannot drift on `DocumentId`, `DocumentUuid`, or observed `ContentVersion`. Acceptance: shared translation logic or a thin shared helper is used where practical for POST and PUT lookup-result-to-target-context conversion; duplicated branching that can diverge on freshness-stamp handling is removed or reduced to minimal wrappers; this task remains scoped to drift prevention and cleanup around target lookup translation, not a broader redesign of repository versus executor ownership.
steps-to-verify: Refactor the repository and executor lookup code so the result-to-target-context translation logic is shared or centralized enough to prevent divergence in `DocumentId`, `DocumentUuid`, and observed `ContentVersion` handling.

Add backend unit tests covering initial lookup and POST re-evaluation with matching assertions for `DocumentId`, `DocumentUuid`, and observed `ContentVersion`, including not-found and existing-document cases.
Search the backend for leftover duplicate target-lookup branching and remove dead wrappers or helper code that no longer serves the runtime path.

category: Eliminate Shared Collection Item Placeholder Tokens
description: Remove or internalize the reusable `FlattenedWriteValue.UnresolvedCollectionItemId.Instance` sentinel so runtime write paths cannot accidentally reuse one placeholder token across multiple inserted rows. Acceptance: production flattening and any other runtime row-buffer creation paths always allocate a unique unresolved collection token per new stable-row-identity reservation site; no public or production-facing API encourages reuse of a singleton placeholder for collection inserts; persistence continues to reserve exactly one `CollectionItemId` per distinct unresolved token and never collapses separate inserted rows onto the same reserved id because they shared a sentinel value; tests that still need a fixed placeholder for isolated contract assertions use an explicit test-local token or another non-production helper instead of the production singleton.
steps-to-verify: Remove, internalize, or otherwise block production use of `FlattenedWriteValue.UnresolvedCollectionItemId.Instance` so the runtime path cannot emit a shared unresolved token for multiple inserted rows.
Audit relational write production code for unresolved collection token creation and ensure every new collection row reservation site uses a fresh token value rather than a singleton placeholder.

Add or update backend unit coverage proving that two new collection rows emitted in one write request carry distinct unresolved tokens and therefore reserve distinct `CollectionItemId` values during persistence.

Add or update backend unit coverage proving aligned scope rows that are supposed to share a base collection identity still share the owning row's token intentionally, while sibling inserted rows do not.
Run: `rg --line-number --no-heading "UnresolvedCollectionItemId\.Instance|UnresolvedCollectionItemId\.Create\(" ./src/dms`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalWriteFlattenerTests|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalWriteNoProfileMergeSynthesizerTests|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalWriteExtensionCollectionMergeSynthesizerTests|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalWriteNonCollectionPersisterTests|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalWriteContractsTests"`

category: Unify Batched DML SQL Generation With Plan Compilation
description: Reduce SQL drift risk by moving batched insert and multi-row update/delete command generation onto the same authoritative compilation/emitter path as single-row write SQL instead of maintaining a second runtime-only SQL-construction path. Acceptance: batched relational write SQL for inserts and repeated update/delete statements is generated from shared compiler/emitter logic or plan-carried templates rather than ad hoc regex-based parameter rewriting in the persister; batching continues to respect dialect-specific identifier quoting and parameter-limit partitioning for PostgreSQL and SQL Server; the runtime persister is simplified so it orchestrates batches without reimplementing SQL-shape knowledge already owned by the plan compiler; tests cover both dialects so batch SQL remains behaviorally aligned with compiled single-row statements when plan emission changes.
steps-to-verify: Refactor batched DML generation so the persister no longer hand-builds a separate SQL dialect surface for batch commands when equivalent compiler or emitter logic can be reused.

Remove or reduce runtime-only SQL text rewriting helpers that are only compensating for duplicated SQL generation, especially regex-based parameter renaming and local identifier quoting logic, unless they remain thin wrappers over shared emitted templates.

Add or update unit coverage proving PostgreSQL and SQL Server batch command generation stays aligned with the same table-plan metadata used for single-row `InsertSql`, `UpdateSql`, `DeleteByParentSql`, and collection-merge statements.
Add or update unit coverage for parameter-limit chunking to show batch partitioning still honors `BulkInsertBatching.MaxRowsPerBatch` and does not regress multi-batch execution behavior.

Run: `rg --line-number --no-heading "BuildParameterizedBatchCommand|BuildInsertBatchCommand|RenameBatchParameters|QuoteIdentifier|BuildInsertBatchSql" ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~WritePlanCompilerMssqlContractTests|FullyQualifiedName~Given_WritePlanCompiler_InsertSql|FullyQualifiedName~Given_WritePlanCompiler_CollectionMerge|FullyQualifiedName~PlanWriteBatchingConventionsTests"`

category: Short-Circuit Missing Existing-Document Read Plans Before Executor Work
description: Fail existing-document relational writes earlier when target lookup resolves an existing document but no compiled relational read plan is available, instead of opening an executor session and resolving references before returning the deterministic guard-rail failure. Acceptance: once repository target lookup determines the request will execute against an existing document, the repository returns the prepared missing-read-plan failure immediately if `ExistingDocumentReadPlan` is unavailable; the executor is not invoked for this scenario; no write transaction is opened and no in-session reference resolution or hydration work is performed for a known guard-rail failure; PUT not-found handling remains unchanged and continues to short-circuit before executor execution; POST create flows without a read-plan dependency still proceed normally.
steps-to-verify: Refactor repository orchestration so existing-document requests without a compiled read plan fail before `IRelationalWriteExecutor.ExecuteAsync(...)` is called.

Add or update repository unit tests for PUT-existing and POST-as-update-existing cases with a missing read plan to assert the repository returns the deterministic unknown failure immediately and does not call the executor.
Add or update executor or repository seam tests to assert reference resolution is not attempted for the missing-read-plan existing-document guard-rail path.
Confirm PUT not-found still short-circuits as `UpdateFailureNotExists` before executor work, and POST create-new still bypasses the existing-document read-plan guard rail.

Run: `rg --line-number --no-heading "PrepareExistingDocumentReadPlan|MissingExistingDocumentReadPlanFailureMessage|TryBuildMissingExistingDocumentReadPlanResult|ExecuteAsync\(" ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepositoryTests|FullyQualifiedName~DefaultRelationalWriteExecutorTests"`

Acceptance: an initial POST creates the document, a second POST with the same referential identity and a different candidate document uuid resolves to the existing document, preserves the original `DocumentId` and `DocumentUuid`, updates root, extension, and child-table state in place, reuses stable collection ids for retained rows, removes omitted rows, inserts replacements, and does not create a second `dms.Document` row for the incoming candidate uuid.

Assert the second POST, using the persisted referential id and a different candidate document uuid, returns update success, preserves the original `DocumentId` and `DocumentUuid`, and updates the existing rowset in place across root, extension, and child tables.

category: Move POST Upsert Existence Resolution Into The Write Session
description: Close the POST create-vs-create race where repository-owned pre-session target lookup can resolve `CreateNew`, then a concurrent writer can insert the same identity before this attempt persists rows. Acceptance: POST upsert does not rely on a pre-session `CreateNew` decision for correctness; existence resolution for the create-vs-existing branch is performed or revalidated inside the executor-owned connection/transaction before any new `dms.Document` row is inserted; when a concurrent writer creates the target between repository orchestration and persistence, the second request follows the POST-as-update path against the existing document instead of inserting a duplicate document row or surfacing an avoidable write-conflict/identity-conflict outcome; successful concurrent POST-as-update preserves the original persisted `DocumentId` and `DocumentUuid`, and repository-level PUT not-found short-circuit behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Refactor POST write orchestration so the create-vs-existing decision that matters for persistence is owned by the executor session rather than by a stale repository lookup result.

Ensure the executor revalidates POST targets before inserting `dms.Document`, and routes a concurrent create into existing-document current-state load, merge, guarded no-op, or persistence as appropriate.

Add backend unit coverage for a POST request whose initial lookup would have resolved `CreateNew`, but whose in-session revalidation finds an existing document; assert the path updates the existing target context and does not call the create-new document insert branch.

Add backend unit or repository seam coverage proving PUT still short-circuits missing targets as `UpdateFailureNotExists` before executor work, and that this refactor does not reintroduce the old PUT-to-create fallback.

Add PostgreSQL integration coverage for two concurrent POST requests with the same referential identity where one writer wins the create and the other arrives before its own persistence step; assert exactly one `dms.Document` row exists afterward, the losing request resolves as update against the existing document, and the persisted root/child rows match last-writer semantics rather than duplicate-document semantics.
